title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eczema
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eczema are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, often taken orally to alleviate eczema symptoms. They help by addressing underlying inflammation, boosting the immune system, and promoting skin health. Examples of effective herbal tinctures include chamomile, calendula, and burdock root. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, calendula promotes skin repair and reduces redness, and burdock root supports liver function, which aids in detoxification. By incorporating these tinctures into their treatment plan, eczema sufferers may experience reduced itching, inflammation, and skin irritation, leading to improved quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, irritation, and infection associated with the skin condition. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, can help alleviate itching, promote wound healing, and strengthen the skin's barrier function. Furthermore, yarrow tincture has been shown to improve skin hydration and elasticity, which can help prevent eczema flare-ups and support overall skin health. Its soothing and calming effects on the skin make it a valuable natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and tannins, helps prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can complicate eczema.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, due to the presence of tannins, helps tighten and dry out weeping eczema, reducing the risk of skin infections.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tincture, due to the presence of volatile oils like azulene, helps alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with eczema.
Immune-modulating: Yarrow tincture's immune-modulating property, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, helps regulate the immune response that contributes to eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help alleviate eczema-related redness and irritation.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and soothing properties, making them ideal for calming eczema-prone skin.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds help restore skin's natural barrier function and reduce eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 ounce of dried yarrow, and chop finely.
Combine herbs with 1 pint of 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean jar, ensuring herbs are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the plant material.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a glass dropper bottle and use as needed to treat eczema symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the skin condition. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, can help soothe inflamed skin, promote tissue regeneration, and enhance the overall health of the skin. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can help alleviate itching, one of the most common and uncomfortable symptoms of eczema, providing relief and improving the quality of life for those suffering from this skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in eczema due to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenes, which inhibit the release of inflammatory chemicals.

Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, important for eczema as it often results in broken and cracked skin, containing active compounds like triterpenes and polysaccharides.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, preventing secondary infections in eczema, attributed to its volatile oils and alkaloids.
Immunomodulatory: The tincture regulates the immune system, reducing overactive immune responses that contribute to eczema, due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Anti-pruritic: Pot marigold tincture alleviates itching, a common symptom of eczema, by inhibiting histamine release, thanks to its flavonoid and tannin content.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers are used to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with eczema.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have been known to possess wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for treating eczema-prone skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils with soothing and moisturizing effects, helping to alleviate dryness and itchiness caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried herbs and place them in a clean glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters full, then pour 80-proof vodka over the herbs until they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herbs.

Transfer the liquid to a clean glass dropper bottle, label it with the name, date, and dosage, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as alpha-bisabolol and apigenin, have been shown to reduce inflammation, ease redness, and alleviate itching associated with eczema. These compounds also exhibit antimicrobial effects, helping to prevent secondary skin infections often seen in eczema patients. Moreover, chamomile has been found to promote skin healing and regeneration, which aids in restoring the skin's natural barrier function. Consequently, incorporating German chamomile tincture into an eczema treatment regimen can help soothe, heal, and protect affected skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of active constituents like alpha-bisabolol and matricin, helps reduce eczema-related redness, swelling, and irritation.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of German chamomile tincture, owing to its chamazulene content, aids in preventing skin infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
Wound-healing: The wound-healing property of German chamomile tincture, attributed to its flavonoid and essential oil content, promotes skin tissue regeneration, benefiting eczema-prone skin.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture's analgesic property, resulting from its alpha-bisabolol content, helps alleviate eczema-related pain and itching, improving overall skin comfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of German chamomile tincture, attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid content, aids in neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, supporting overall skin health and resilience against eczema triggers.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing infection in eczema.
Leaves: The leaves have a soothing effect on the skin, and can help alleviate itching and irritation caused by eczema.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which can help reduce redness and dryness associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the herbs until they are fully submerged.
Cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid, and discard the spent herbs.
Transfer the finished tincture to a clean glass bottle, label it with the ingredients and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) daily for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with eczema because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties can help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation associated with eczema. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help prevent infection in broken or irritated skin. The tincture form allows for easy absorption and quick relief of symptoms. By using eastern purple coneflower tincture, individuals with eczema may experience improved skin texture, reduced itchiness, and overall better skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for eczema as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and itching. This property is attributed to the presence of echinacoside and cichoric acid.
Antimicrobial: The tincture helps prevent infection of eczema-prone skin by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses, thanks to the active constituents like alkamides and polysaccharides.

Wound healing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes skin tissue regeneration, which is crucial for eczema treatment as it aids in the repair of damaged skin. The active compounds responsible for this property include alkamides, flavonoids, and polysaccharides.

Immune system support: The tincture boosts the immune system, which is important for eczema as it helps the body fight off infections and reduces inflammation. The key active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides and alkamides.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for eczema as it helps maintain skin health and reduces inflammation, with active constituents such as flavonoids, cichoric acid, and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help soothe irritated skin in eczema.
Leaves: Containing beneficial flavonoids, the leaves aid in reducing redness and itching associated with eczema.
Stems: High in antioxidants, the stems help support skin health and alleviate eczema symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, the seeds provide moisture and nourishment for eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry basket.
Chop or grind 2 cups of fresh flowers, then combine with 2 cups of 100-proof vodka in a glass jar, sealing tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding plant material and storing the liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL, three times daily).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi. Eczema is a skin condition characterized by red, itchy, and inflamed patches, and St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing. Additionally, St. John's wort has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. By addressing the underlying inflammation and promoting skin health, St. John's wort tincture can provide relief from eczema symptoms and support overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture helps reduce eczema-related inflammation, soothing irritated skin. This property is due to the active constituents hypericin and hyperforin.
Antimicrobial: The tincture can inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting skin health. This is attributed to the presence of hyperforin.
Wound-healing: St. John's wort tincture promotes skin tissue regeneration and wound healing, alleviating eczema-related lesions. Hypericin and hyperforin contribute to this medicinal property.
Analgesic: The tincture can help relieve eczema-related pain and itching, improving overall skin comfort. Hypericin and flavonoids are responsible for this analgesic effect.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tincture contains antioxidants that protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, supporting skin health and reducing eczema symptoms. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids, hypericin, and hyperforin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe eczema symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, that have been shown to help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with eczema.
Stems: Contain similar medicinal properties as the flowers and leaves, providing a natural remedy for eczema symptoms.
Rhyzomes: While not as commonly used as the above parts, rhyzomes also contain medicinal compounds that can help alleviate eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar to 3/4 full.
Add 80-proof vodka to the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged and leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the liquid in a glass dropper bottle.
Apply 2-3 drops of the St. John's Wort tincture directly to eczema-affected areas, up to three times a day, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. It contains allantoin, which promotes skin cell regeneration and helps to soothe irritated skin. Plantain tincture also has a high mucilage content, which forms a protective layer on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce redness and itching associated with eczema. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation, while its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection. Regular use of plantain tincture can help to alleviate the symptoms of eczema and promote healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for eczema as it is characterized by inflamed, itchy, and red skin. Active constituents such as allantoin, flavonoids, and mucilage contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties.

Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for eczema as it can help repair damaged skin and reduce the risk of infection. Allantoin and tannins in plantain stimulate skin cell regeneration and have astringent properties.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture has antimicrobial properties, which are essential for eczema as it can help prevent skin infections caused by scratching and breaking the skin. Active compounds like aucubin and flavonoids have antimicrobial effects.

Anti-pruritic: Plantain tincture alleviates itching, which is important for eczema as it helps break the itch-scratch cycle. The iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin, in plantain have anti-pruritic properties.

Demulcent: Plantain tincture has a soothing effect on the skin, which is beneficial for eczema as it can help alleviate discomfort and dryness. Mucilage, a polysaccharide found in plantain, forms a protective layer on the skin, helping to retain moisture and reduce irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, plantain leaves help soothe itchy, inflamed skin associated with eczema.
Seeds: Plantain seeds contain mucilage, which can help reduce irritation and promote healing of eczema-prone skin.
Flowers: Plantain flowers have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, aiding in the treatment of eczema and potential infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest Plantain: Gather 2-3 cups of fresh Plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free area, ensuring they are fully grown and free from damage.
Clean and Chop: Rinse the leaves thoroughly, let them dry, then chop them coarsely, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to three-quarters full.
Prepare Tincture: Add 80-proof vodka or rum to the jar, covering the leaves completely, leaving a 1-inch space at the top, and sealing the jar tightly.

Steep and Strain: Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it occasionally. After steeping, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean jar or dark glass bottle, discarding the leaves.
Label and Store: Label the tincture with the contents, date, and dosage (20-30 drops, 2-3 times daily), and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common knotweed tincture helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin condition. The herb's active compounds, including resveratrol and emodin, can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. Moreover, common knotweed tincture can help to strengthen the skin's barrier function, preventing the entry of allergens and irritants that can trigger eczema flare-ups. Its soothing and calming effects on the skin make it an effective natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Common knotweed tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of resveratrol and other polyphenols, helps reduce eczema-related redness, swelling, and itching.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from resveratrol, aids in neutralizing free radicals, which helps alleviate eczema-induced oxidative stress and skin damage.
Antimicrobial: The tincture's antimicrobial property, attributed to emodin and other compounds, helps prevent secondary skin infections, a common complication of eczema.

Immunomodulatory: Knotweed tincture's ability to modulate the immune response, thanks to its polyphenolic constituents, can help regulate the overactive immune system that contributes to eczema symptoms.

Collagen synthesis: The tincture's capacity to promote collagen synthesis, due to the presence of resveratrol, supports skin healing, regeneration, and strengthening, which is beneficial for eczema-ravaged skin.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of common knotweed, rich in resveratrol and effective in reducing inflammation and irritation in eczema.
Leaves: Packed with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, knotweed leaves help soothe and heal eczema-prone skin.
Stems: High in flavonoids, stems of common knotweed provide relief from itching and swelling associated with eczema.
Buds: Bursting with therapeutic compounds, buds help alleviate symptoms of eczema by promoting skin repair and reducing redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest common knotweed (Polygonum aviculare) shoots, ensuring they have not yet flowered, and rinse them thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of fresh knotweed into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar, filling it up to 3/4.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the chopped knotweed, ensuring all plant material is submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using a cheesecloth or muslin, squeezing out the liquid, and store it in a dark glass bottle with a dropper. Apply a few drops of the tincture directly to the affected eczema area, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silverweed tincture helps with eczema because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the skin condition. The herb's tannins and flavonoids have been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, helping to alleviate itching and dryness. Additionally, silverweed tincture may help to strengthen the skin's barrier function, reducing the likelihood of eczema flare-ups. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to prevent the activation of immune cells that can contribute to eczema symptoms. Overall, silverweed tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Silverweed tincture helps reduce eczema inflammation, soothing irritated skin and alleviating redness, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of tannins, help prevent skin infections caused by bacteria, fungi, or viruses that can worsen eczema symptoms.
Antioxidant: Silverweed tincture's antioxidant effects, due to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids, protect skin cells from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall skin health.

Astringent: The astringent property of silverweed tincture, caused by tannins, helps tighten and dry out oozing or weeping eczema lesions, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of skin infections.
Analgesic: Silverweed tincture's analgesic properties, resulting from its various active constituents, help alleviate eczema-related pain and itchiness, providing relief and promoting skin comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe irritated skin and alleviate eczema symptoms.
Rhizomes: High in antioxidants, rhizomes aid in skin repair and help reduce eczema-related redness and inflammation.
Leaves: Loaded with soothing properties, leaves help moisturize and alleviate itching, making them ideal for eczema tea.
Flowers: Containing anti-bacterial and anti-fungal agents, flowers help prevent eczema-related infections and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silverweed plant material, ensuring it is free from pesticides and other contaminants, then rinse and dry thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried silverweed into small pieces and place in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or other food-grade alcohol over the silverweed, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily to promote even extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent plant material, and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping buttercup tincture helps with eczema because it contains anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin condition. The herb's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, while its antipruritic properties can help soothe itching and minimize scratching. Moreover, creeping buttercup tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections that may occur due to broken skin. Its demulcent properties can also help moisturize and protect the skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring. Overall, creeping buttercup tincture can provide relief from eczema symptoms and support the skin's overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Creeping buttercup tincture helps reduce inflammation in eczema due to the presence of isorhamnetin, an anti-inflammatory flavonoid.
Antipruritic: The tincture alleviates itching in eczema, thanks to the antipruritic properties of runtotricoside and other saponins found in the plant.
Antimicrobial: Creeping buttercup tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, such as Staphylococcus aureus, commonly found in eczema lesions, due to its antimicrobial properties.
Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing in eczema by stimulating tissue regeneration and reducing scarring, owing to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids.

Immunomodulatory: Creeping buttercup tincture helps balance the immune response in eczema, reducing hypersensitivity and inflammation, due to its immunomodulatory properties, attributed to various constituents, including isorhamnetin and ranunculin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe eczema symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, helping reduce redness and irritation caused by eczema.
Rhizomes: Packed with tannins, these can help alleviate itching and dryness associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest creeping buttercup (Petasites hybridus) aerial parts, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants, during the flowering stage.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of the harvested herb to increase its surface area, then place it in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged, then secure the jar with an airtight lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, then store it in a dark glass dropper bottle for up to 2 years. Apply the tincture topically to affected eczema areas, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed tincture helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-itch properties that can alleviate the redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. Its soothing effect on the skin can reduce irritation and promote healing. Chickweed is also rich in essential nutrients, such as vitamins A, C, and B, as well as minerals like potassium and calcium, which can help strengthen the skin's barrier function and protect it from external triggers. Furthermore, chickweed has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent secondary infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Overall, chickweed tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chickweed tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids.
Wound healing: Its wound healing properties help eczema by promoting tissue regeneration and reducing the risk of infection, thanks to the action of polysaccharides and tannins.
Anti-pruritic: Chickweed tincture alleviates itching, a common eczema symptom, because of the anti-pruritic effects of triterpenoid saponins and essential oils.
Demulcent: The demulcent properties of chickweed tincture soothe irritated skin, forming a protective barrier and reducing discomfort, due to the mucilaginous compounds found in the plant.
Antimicrobial: Chickweed tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections in eczema-prone skin, as a result of the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, chickweed leaves help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with eczema.
Stems: Containing antihistamine and vasoconstrictive properties, chickweed stems help alleviate itching and swelling in eczema-prone skin.
Flowers: High in flavonoids and carotenoids, chickweed flowers possess potent antioxidant properties that support skin health and healing in eczema.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, chickweed seeds aid in maintaining skin moisture and reducing inflammation in eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chickweed, filling a clean jar halfway with the herb, leaving at least 1-inch of space at the top.
Combine the chickweed with 80-proof vodka, ensuring the herb is fully submerged, then secure the lid and shake gently.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the chickweed solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, amber glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place or refrigerator for up to one year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eczema with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing herbs. For instance, a tincture made from calendula, chamomile, and licorice root can help reduce inflammation, redness, and itching associated with eczema. Another effective tincture can be made from burdock, cleavers, and nettle, which have blood purifying and skin healing properties. These tinctures should be taken orally, 2-3 times a day, and can also be applied topically to the affected areas. However, it's important to consult a healthcare practitioner before starting any new herbal treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal tinctures include psoriasis and dermatitis. These skin conditions often result in red, itchy, and inflamed skin. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from calendula, chamomile, and burdock, can help alleviate symptoms. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing irritated skin. Chamomile contains flavonoids and essential oils that possess anti-inflammatory and antiphlogistic properties, helping to reduce redness and irritation. Burdock has been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, as it has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. These herbal tinctures can be applied directly to the skin or taken orally, depending on the specific instructions of a healthcare provider.
